comparison.feed: BMTC – Bengaluru Metropolitan Transport Corporation (agencies BMTC-Regular, BMTC-AC)

breaking_changes.detail: 9 of 23 named service_ids from the old feed are absent in the new feed (14 common, 0 added): 'Fri-Sat', 'Fri-Sun', 'Holiday&Saturday', 'Mon-Tue', 'Sat-Sun', 'Sun-Mon', 'Test_Wed', 'Thu-Fri', 'Tue-Wed'. service_ids are stable human-readable names, so these are real service-pattern removals (-39.1%).
breaking_changes.note: VERIFIED CLEAN: none of the 9 removed service_ids are referenced by the new trips.txt (0 orphan foreign keys) — the new schedule was re-pointed onto the 14 surviving services. Any external system that referenced the dropped service_ids (e.g. 'Sat-Sun') breaks.

breaking_changes.detail: 9,621 of 84,391 trip_ids (11.4%) from the old feed are absent in the new feed (74,770 common, 3,370 added). trip_ids are stable composite keys (e.g. '328HS/4_1_Regular_4'), so their disappearance is a genuine deletion.
breaking_changes.note: Internal integrity is intact — every new stop_times.trip_id resolves against new trips (0 missing). The removals still break any external reference to the 9,621 old trip_ids.

breaking_changes.detail: 1,045 of 12,264 route_ids (8.5%) from the old feed are absent in the new feed (11,219 common, 755 added). route_ids are stable descriptive keys (e.g. '104_BSMCL2_KBS3_DOWN') and all 11,219 common routes keep an identical route_long_name, confirming these are real route removals, not ID reassignments.
breaking_changes.note: Internal integrity is intact — every new trips.route_id and fare_rules.route_id resolves against new routes (0 missing).

breaking_changes.detail: 43 of 12,235 stop_ids (0.35%) from the old feed are absent in the new feed, with 43 added (total stop count unchanged at 12,235). stop_ids are stable, so this is a small genuine churn of individual stops.
breaking_changes.note: VERIFIED CLEAN: every new stop_times.stop_id resolves against new stops (0 missing); none of the 43 removed stop_ids are referenced by the new schedule.

suspicious_changes.detail: calendar rows dropped 23 → 14 (-39.1%) and calendar_dates rows dropped 368 → 224 (-39.1%), both far exceeding the 20% threshold. Driven by the 9 removed service patterns above.
suspicious_changes.note: Consistent and intentional — a service-calendar consolidation between two weekly snapshots (2026-03-03 → 2026-03-10). All other files stay within threshold (routes -2.4%, trips -7.4%, stop_times -7.3%, fare_rules -2.2%, stops 0%, fare_attributes 0%).

suspicious_changes.detail: 2,032 of 12,192 common stops (16.7%) changed their stop_code value, while stop_name, stop_lat, stop_lon and zone_id were left unchanged for 100% of common stops.
suspicious_changes.note: An identifier-style field (stop_code) was re-coded in bulk without moving the stops. stop_code is optional and not a primary key, so referential integrity is unaffected, but a consumer keying on stop_code rather than stop_id would mismatch ~1 in 6 stops.

checks_passed.removed_file: none — both feeds contain the identical 9 files (agency, calendar, calendar_dates, fare_attributes, fare_rules, routes, stop_times, stops, trips); 0 added, 0 removed
checks_passed.removed_required_field: none — every file's CSV header is byte-identical between the two feeds; 0 columns added or deleted in any file
checks_passed.changed_field_type_or_format: none — all 1,902,591 new stop_times rows have valid HH:MM:SS arrival/departure times (0 malformed, 0 empty arrivals)
checks_passed.referential_integrity_new_feed: clean — stop_times→stops 0 missing, stop_times→trips 0 missing, trips→routes 0 missing, trips→service (calendar+calendar_dates) 0 missing, fare_rules→routes 0 missing, fare_rules→fare_attributes 0 missing
checks_passed.enum_values: identical: route_type {3}, exception_type {1,2}, payment_method {0}; fare_attributes.txt is byte-identical (36 fares unchanged)
checks_passed.feed_validity_window: unchanged — calendar window 2026-01-01 → 2026-12-31 (full year; end date in the future relative to 2026-03-10)
checks_passed.service_coverage_gap: none — identical full-year service window, complete overlap
checks_passed.entity_count_deltas: calendar -39.1% and calendar_dates -39.1% exceed 20% (flagged above); routes -2.4%, trips -7.4%, stop_times -7.3%, fare_rules -2.2%, stops 0%, fare_attributes 0% within threshold
checks_passed.stops_moved_over_500m: 0 of 12,192 common stops — all common stops have unchanged coordinates
checks_passed.headsign_route_name_bulk_change: 0% — route_long_name unchanged for all 11,219 common routes; stop_name unchanged for all 12,192 common stops (this feed has no trip_headsign field)
checks_passed.trip_route_reassignment: 1,535 of 74,770 common trips (2.1%) were re-pointed to a different route_id; service_id unchanged for 100% of common trips — below a bulk-change concern
checks_passed.calendar_flip: no — both feeds use calendar.txt + calendar_dates.txt with the same modeling strategy
checks_passed.agency_timezone_change: no — agency.txt is byte-identical (BMTC-Regular, BMTC-AC; Asia/Kolkata)
checks_passed.duplicate_ids: none — 0 duplicate stop_ids, route_ids, or trip_ids in either feed
checks_passed.stop_time_ordering: 0 violations across all 1,902,591 new rows (stop_sequence strictly increasing, departure ≥ arrival, arrival ≥ prior departure)
